rationale: X's blocker finding pinpoints that the unconditional NotImplementedError guard breaks five specific pre-existing sleep(level=2)→wake_up() tests, which is exactly the failure mode the latent-gap check describes (the real post-merge CI break in #4905, later relaxed in #4912) — a direct, well-evidenced hit. Y's adjacent finding about the sleep() default level=2 'bricking' the engine is plausible but more speculative (the default value isn't shown in the diff) and never checks whether existing tests/CI already exercise the guarded path, so it misses the precise gap. Y covers the two thin ground-truth threads (regression-test adequacy, enum design) more directly than X's brief sweep notes, giving it a modest recall edge, but X's findings are overall more concrete, grounded, and scannable (explicit file:line, severity tags, verifiable via grep).
